renewal_stable.rationale: Stable renewal — 2025 edition model (base fee + tiered users) is the new default. Block pricing as fallback for legacy contracts.

renewal_audit_averse.rationale: Client wants zero audit friction — 2025 model reduces discountable components to additional users only. AYCE eliminates counting entirely.

renewal_with_expansion.rationale: Edition upgrade (Essential → Premium) or bundle new modules into renewal for ARR uplift.

expansion_budget_tight.rationale: Budget-constrained expansion — ramp delays full cost until adoption proves value. Floor protects Backbase.

expansion_multi_segment.rationale: Different segments have different economics — dual-track keeps pricing clean.

new_logo_standard.rationale: New logos should default to the 2025 edition model. Fixed base fee + tiered users provides simplicity and embedded hosting.

new_logo_competitive.rationale: Win the deal with competitive entry pricing via ramp, or use 2025 edition with lower tier for entry.

new_logo_large.rationale: Large new logo — 2025 edition model scales well with tiered pricing. Floor/kicker protects long-term ARR.

upsell_edition_upgrade.rationale: Upsell from Essential to Premium or Signature edition — natural upgrade path with clear feature differentiation.

upsell_premium.rationale: Upsell to premium modules — bundle for optics and margin protection.

high_growth_market.rationale: Rapid user growth expected — floor protects base, kicker shares upside. 2025 tiered model scales naturally.
